commonAnnotations.description: app_db_conn_in_use for {{SVC}} reached 100 against a max pool size of 100 (100% utilization), above the 80 SLO. New queries are blocking on connection acquisition and timing out; checked-out connections are not being returned to the pool (leak).

adversarial_signals: The backing Postgres dependency (cart-db) is healthy: app_db_query_latency_p99 is 11ms and app_db_up=1, so this is not a slow or down database — connections are exhausted at the client pool, not blocked on the server.

{{SVC}} rolled out a new image tag (cart:3.4.1) 6 hours ago, well before the incident window, tempting a bad_deploy conclusion — but the rollout completed cleanly and the pool started saturating only after a traffic ramp, not at the rollout.

{{SVC}} pods log occasional 'context deadline exceeded' calling cart-db, suggesting dependency_failure — but the deadline is hit waiting to ACQUIRE a pooled connection (wait queue), not on the query itself; cart-db responds fast to the few queries that get a connection.
Node {{NODE}} and all nodes are Ready with ample CPU/memory headroom, and pod cpu (0.28 cores) and memory (44%) are nominal, so this is not resource_exhaustion or node_failure.

pods.name: {{POD}}
pods.note: container is Ready and not restarting — the failure is in-process pool saturation, not a crash or OOM

events.message: Readiness probe failed: HTTP 503 from /healthz/ready (db pool acquire timeout) — probe flapping but container still Running
Readiness probe failed: HTTP 503 from /healthz/ready (db pool acquire timeout)
Scaled up replica set {{WORKLOAD}}-7b6b9dfb7c to 3 (image cart:3.4.1) — rollout complete 6h before incident (adversarial)
cart-db probe OK: TCP connect 1ms, SELECT 1 returned in 3ms (adversarial — backing DB is healthy)

lines: 2026-06-20T10:12:38Z INFO  db pool stats: in_use=92 idle=0 max=100 wait_queue=14
2026-06-20T10:12:40Z WARN  db pool near capacity: in_use=98/100 idle=0 pending_acquires=31
2026-06-20T10:12:41Z ERROR DBPoolExhausted: cart db pool saturated 100/100
2026-06-20T10:12:41Z ERROR connection acquire timed out after 2000ms: context deadline exceeded (pool=cart-db in_use=100 max=100)
2026-06-20T10:12:41Z ERROR returning 503 to client: could not obtain db connection (cart pool exhausted)
2026-06-20T10:13:05Z WARN  leaked connection detected: checked out for 47s without release, stmt=SELECT * FROM cart_items WHERE cart_id=$1
2026-06-20T10:18:55Z ERROR DBPoolExhausted: cart db pool saturated 100/100

nodes.note: Ready with headroom — failure is application pool-scoped, not node-scoped (adversarial)

DB server responds fast for queries that get a connection — backing DB is healthy (adversarial)
cart-db reachable and serving (adversarial — not dependency_failure)

annotations.description: app_db_conn_in_use 100 >= 95% of max pool 100; pending_acquires>30 and acquire timeouts firing

answer.model_response:
ROOT_CAUSE: The {{SVC}} service has exhausted its database connection pool: app_db_conn_in_use is pinned at 100/100 (max pool size) across {{POD}}, {{POD2}}, and {{POD3}}, with 30+ pending acquires and a leaked connection held 47s without release. New queries block waiting for a connection and time out after 2000ms (context deadline exceeded on acquire), so {{SVC}} returns 503s. app_db_conn_in_use is at 100 vs the 80 SLO. This is connection-pool saturation (a leak / unbounded concurrency against a fixed pool), not a slow or failed backing DB.
ROOT_CAUSE_CATEGORY: saturation
VALIDATED_CLAIMS:
- app_db_conn_in_use=100/100 (100% pool utilization) on {{POD}} and {{POD2}}, ramping 47->76->94->100 over 4m, breaching the 80 SLO [evidence: prometheus_metrics]
- Pod log 'DBPoolExhausted: cart db pool saturated 100/100' and 'connection acquire timed out after 2000ms: context deadline exceeded (pool=cart-db in_use=100 max=100)' [evidence: k8s_pod_logs]
- Pod log 'leaked connection detected: checked out for 47s without release' — connections are not returned to the pool [evidence: k8s_pod_logs]
- app_db_conn_acquire_timeout_total rate 28.4/s and wait_queue 30+, with http_request_error_rate 0.41 from acquire-timeout 503s [evidence: prometheus_metrics]
- DBPoolExhausted alert firing since 10:12:30 with value 100 [evidence: prometheus_alerts]
- Readiness probes on {{POD}} and {{POD2}} flapping with HTTP 503 from /healthz/ready (db pool acquire timeout) while containers stay Running — a pool acquire timeout, not a crash [evidence: k8s_events]
NON_VALIDATED_CLAIMS:
- Not a dependency_failure: app_db_query_latency_p99=11ms, app_db_up=1, and the cart-db probe is OK (TCP 1ms, SELECT 1 in 3ms) — the backing DB is fast; the timeout is on acquiring a pooled connection, not on the query [evidence: prometheus_metrics, k8s_events]
- Not a bad deploy: the cart:3.4.1 rollout completed cleanly 6h before the incident and all 3 replicas are Available [evidence: k8s_deployments, k8s_events]
- Not resource_exhaustion or node_failure: pods have 0 restarts, cpu 0.28 cores and memory 44% are nominal, and all nodes are Ready with headroom [evidence: k8s_pods, prometheus_metrics, k8s_node_health]
CAUSAL_CHAIN:
- A connection leak / unbounded concurrency checks out cart-db connections faster than they are released -> in_use climbs to the fixed max pool of 100 -> the pool is saturated and new requests queue for a connection -> acquires time out after 2000ms (context deadline exceeded) and {{SVC}} returns 503 -> app_db_conn_in_use breaches the 80 SLO and the DBPoolExhausted alert fires -> restarting the {{SVC}} deployment drops every leaked connection and resets in_use, bringing the pool back under SLO.
